data.title: Metis to Host OpenClaw Hack in Toronto on March 28
data.description:
Metis will host OpenClaw Hack in Toronto on March 28 together with GOAT Network. The event is presented as a one-day AI agent sprint with Mac Minis, internships, and continuation opportunities for selected projects.

METIS Info

Metis is a Layer 2 Rollup platform built on the Ethereum network that aims to tackle some of the key challenges faced by the main net Ethereum — namely transaction speed, cost, and scalability. It achieves this by offering a framework for simple, rapid deployment of smart contracts within the network, thereby facilitating seamless and efficient operations on the Ethereum blockchain.

At the core of Metis’s offerings are several key features: Metis Oracles, Metis Nodes, and Metis SubGraph. Metis Oracles provide real-time data from external sources, connecting blockchains to external systems and supplying smart contracts with crucial outside information. Metis Nodes, which store a complete copy of the distributed network, enhance security and reliability. Lastly, the Metis SubGraph serves as an indexing protocol for gathering, processing, and storing data for use in the blockchain network, aiming to ease information retrieval through a service known as GraphQL. These combined features position Metis as a significant player in improving the functionality and performance of the Ethereum network.

METIS is an ERC-20 governance token compatible with the Ethereum blockchain network. The main use cases for the METIS token are paying transaction fees on the Metis network and staking.

data.title: ADNOC and OMV appoint new leadership for Borouge Group International
data.description:
Abu Dhabi state oil giant ADNOC and Austria's OMV said on Thursday they have appointed Roger Kearns as chief executive officer and Sultan Al Jaber as chairman of the supervisory board for Borouge Group International, a new global polyolefins company from an expected merger.

BGI is being formed through the combination of ADNOC and OMV's shareholdings in Borouge and Borealis, alongside the acquisition of NOVA Chemicals. The transactions are on track to close before the end of March 2026.

Kearns, currently president and CEO of NOVA Chemicals, has over 40 years of experience in the chemicals sector, ADNOC and OMV said in a statement.

Al Jaber is the managing director and group CEO of ADNOC, as well as the executive chairman of XRG, ADNOC's international investment arm. He is also the United Arab Emirates' minister of industry and advanced technology and was president of the COP28 climate summit hosted by his country.

BGI's broader executive leadership team draws directly from the merging companies. Borealis CEO Stefan Doboczky will be chief commercial officer of BGI. Hasan Karam, who has served as chief operating officer of Borouge Plc since May 2023, will take on the same role for the new group.

Borealis CFO Daniel Turnheim has been named interim chief financial officer until a permanent replacement is appointed by May 2026.

BGI will be jointly owned by OMV and XRG, with each holding an equal 50% share. BGI will hold approximately 90% of Borouge, which will retain its listing on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange until a proposed tender offer converts its shares into Borouge Group International shares, with current CEO Hazeem Sultan Al Suwaidi remaining in his post during the transition.

data.title: Diesel markets rebound, some April buying interest resurfaces
data.description:
Asia's diesel markets rebounded on Thursday, though window spot activity remained thin for a second straight session, with some sporadic buying interest emerging from regional buyers.

Vietnam's Petrolimex sought April spot cargoes via a tender that closes tomorrow.

Refining margins (GO10SGCKMc1) increased to around $62 a barrel, paring some of the losses in the past two sessions.

Meanwhile, cash differentials (GO10-SIN-DIF) tracked the firmer paper market structure, rising slightly to around $52.40 a barrel.

Jet fuel markets were still factoring in some demand destruction going into April, with a few regional airlines starting to cut flights, especially in Vietnam.

Meanwhile, traders are expecting supplies to be sufficient until April at least, given the redirection of cargoes from swing suppliers from two weeks ago, which could weigh on jet fuel paper markets in the near term.

Regrade (JETREG10SGMc1) recovered slightly from the previous session, closing at discounts of around 61 cents a barrel.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

- No deal for gasoil or jet fuel

INVENTORIES

- U.S. crude stocks rose more than expected and distillate inventories posted a surprise jump, the Energy Information Administration said on Wednesday. Crude inventories rose by 6.9 million barrels to 456.2 million barrels in the week ended March 20, the highest since June 2024, the EIA said. This compared with analysts' expectations in a Reuters poll for a 477,000-barrel rise.

- Singapore's inventories of middle distillates rose to 9 million barrels as net exports of diesel fell, although an increase in jet fuel net exports slowed the gain, official government data showed on Thursday.

NEWS

- South Korea will raise a cap on fuel prices effective Friday at midnight and expand fuel tax breaks to ease the burden on consumers dealing with the fallout from the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, the government said on Thursday.

- France's Gravenchon refinery operated by North Atlantic has agreed to increase production of specific fuels by 10%, after talks between the French government and refiners over the current energy market situation, Delegate Minister for the French Energy Ministry Maud Bregeon said.

- The Philippine energy ministry said on Thursday it is activating a 20 billion peso ($333 million) emergency fund to strengthen fuel security amid continued volatility in oil prices due to the conflict in the Middle East.

- At least 40% of Russia's oil export capacity is at a halt following Ukrainian drone attacks, a disputed attack on a major pipeline and the seizure of tankers, according to Reuters calculations based on market data.

data.title: China's Ping An Insurance posts 6.45% profit rise on life insurance growth
data.description:
Key points:
  • Ping An posts 134.778 bln yuan net profit, under forecasts
  • Life insurance new business value jumps 29.3% on product shift
  • Insurer warns external environment impact will heighten amid economic challenges

China's Ping An Insurance reported a 6.45% year-on-year rise in net profit in 2025, driven by growth in new business value margin and equity investment gains, according to a filing on Thursday.

The insurance company posted 134.778 billion yuan ($19.51 billion) in net profit last year, below a median estimate of 136.774 billion yuan, according to LSEG data. Operating profit rose 10.3% in 2025.

New business value (NBV) in its life and health insurance segment, which measures the profitability of new policies sold, grew 29.3% year-on-year to 36.897 billion yuan.

The insurer's NBV growth reflects a broader industry shift toward higher-margin protection products after years of declining agent productivity and weak consumer demand.

Still, Ping An warned that the "impact from the changing external environment will heighten" and the domestic economy is facing certain challenges in the short term.

The number of retail customers rose 3.5% to 250.97 million by end-December from a year earlier, the filing showed.

Ping An's insurance funds investment portfolio grew 13.2% to 6.49 trillion yuan as of December from the beginning of 2025, the filing showed.

The insurer's investment performance benefited from a rally in Chinese equities as authorities strengthened support for capital markets, including measures introduced in early 2025 aimed at channelling hundreds of billions of yuan from major insurers into stocks.

China's push into artificial intelligence and advanced technologies drew global investors back to the market, which helped sustain bullish sentiment through 2025.

Banking unit Ping An Bank last week reported its net profit in 2025 fell 4.2% year-on-year as pressure on interest margin persists.

($1 = 6.9066 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: Next PLC's Shares Rise After Sales Outlook Confirmation Despite Possible Hit From Iran War
data.description:
By Andrea Figueras

Shares in Next PLC jumped after the U.K. clothing retailer maintained its fiscal-year sales-growth expectations, despite warning that the Iran war could affect costs, prices and consumer demand.

The group said Thursday it had accounted for 15 million pounds ($20 million) in additional costs--including fuel and air freight--tied to the Middle East conflict. The costs didn't affect Next's guidance since they have been offset by savings, it said.

For the fiscal year through January 2027, Next continues to anticipate an increase in full-price sales of 4.5% to 5.9 billion pounds. Pretax profit is expected at 1.21 billion pounds compared with a prior forecast of 1.20 billion pounds.

In morning trading, London-listed shares were up 5.5% at 127 pounds. The stock has dropped 4.6% so far this year.

"If the conflict persists, the costs are likely to be reflected in higher prices to consumers and disruption to our supply chain, both of which are likely to suppress sales," the company said. Instability in the Middle East, which represents around 6% of the group's total turnover, might continue to restrain growth in that region.

The group, regarded as a bellwether for the British retail sector, plans to give more detailed information about the impact of the war in May, when it expects to have a clearer picture of its long-term implications.

Next said sales in the first eight weeks of the year--before the start of the conflict--were encouraging in and outside of the U.K. "We see this update as reassuring on the strong U.K. trading and implied ability to pass-through costs," Jefferies analysts said in a research note.

Meanwhile, the group reported that total group sales in fiscal 2026 rose 11% to 7 billion pounds on year. Pretax profit grew 14% to 1.16 billion pounds.

"The numbers underline the group's unparalleled understanding of the market in which it operates and its ability to capitalize on new opportunities," Interactive Investor's Richard Hunter wrote in a note.

Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com

data.title: Shoe Carnival Q4 sales slightly beat estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. footwear retailer's Q4 sales slightly beat analyst expectations

  • Fiscal 2025 net sales fell 5.6%, with Shoe Station banner sales up 2.7% organically

  • In March 2026, Board approved a raise in quarterly dividend to $0.17 per share, marking 12th consecutive annual increase

Outlook

  • Shoe Carnival sees fiscal 2026 net sales about flat, ranging from down 1% to up 1%

  • Company expects fiscal 2026 gross profit margin of about 34%, down 260 basis points from 2025

  • Shoe Carnival expects fiscal 2026 adjusted EPS of $1.40 to $1.60

Result Drivers

  • LOWER-INCOME PRESSURE - Co said Shoe Carnival banner sales declined due to continued pressure on lower-income consumers and reduced promotional marketing

  • REBANNER INVESTMENTS - Co said rebanner investments reduced Q4 EPS by about $0.08, mainly impacting selling, general and administrative expenses

  • MERCHANDISE MARGIN EXPANSION - Co said merchandise margin expanded 30 basis points in Q4, offset by higher buying, distribution, and occupancy costs

data.title: OpenAI indefinitely pauses plans to release erotic chatbot, FT says
data.description:
OpenAI has shelved plans to release an erotic chatbot indefinitely as it looks to focus on its core products, according to a report by the Financial Times on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Employees and investors have raised concerns about the effect of sexualized AI content on society, the report said.

OpenAI has also abruptly canceled Sora, a text-to-video model, and is now looking to focus on other research areas and rolling out more of its capabilities into a single super-app.

The company did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.

data.title: Rockwell Medical posts $69.3M sales in 2025, fourth-quarter revenue $18.3M and positive Adjusted EBITDA
data.description:
Rockwell Medical reported fourth-quarter 2025 net sales of $18.3 million and full-year net sales of $69.3 million, with the company achieving profitability on an Adjusted EBITDA basis for the second consecutive year. The company recorded a 21% gross margin in Q4 2025 (one of its strongest quarters) and ended the year with $25.0 million in cash and investments. Net loss for full-year 2025 was $5.3 million while Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $0.3 million.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: Net sales of $18.3 million for the three months ended Dec. 31, 2025; $69.3 million for the twelve months ended Dec. 31, 2025.
  • Gross profit and margin: Q4 2025 gross profit $3.9 million with a 21% gross margin; full-year 2025 gross profit $11.7 million with a 17% gross margin.
  • Operating result: Operating loss of $0.4 million for Q4 2025 and operating loss of $4.7 million for full-year 2025 (as reported).
  • Net income (loss): Net loss of $0.6 million for Q4 2025 and net loss of $5.3 million for full-year 2025.
  • Non-GAAP and per-share: Adjusted EBITDA of $1.0 million for Q4 2025 and $0.3 million for full-year 2025; Adjusted EPS of $0.02 for Q4 2025 and $0.01 for full-year 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Customer and contract activity: Extended an amended product purchase agreement with a large U.S. dialysis provider through Dec. 31, 2026, with increased pricing for the extended term.
  • Distribution expansion: Added 30 new customers in the western U.S.; western region now represents more than 10% of the company’s clinic footprint and improves delivery capabilities in that region.
  • Product portfolio and regulatory: Added a 510(k)-cleared single-use bicarbonate cartridge (two sizes: 720g and 900g) to the hemodialysis concentrates portfolio in February 2025.
  • Leadership and operations: Appointed Joe Dawson to the board, promoted Heather Hunter to Chief Operating Officer, and named Rashad Brown VP of Manufacturing and Supply Chain during 2025; completed infrastructure adjustments to align operations with demand.
  • Liquidity and cash flow: Generated $2.3 million in operating cash flow in Q4 2025, increasing cash and investments to $25.0 million at year-end.

Original SEC Filing: ROCKWELL MEDICAL, INC. [ RMTI ] - 8-K - Mar. 26, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Equitable and Corebridge Agree to All-Stock Merger; HoldCo Ownership Split 51%/49%
data.description:
Equitable Holdings entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger with Corebridge Financial to combine in an all-stock transaction under a new parent company, HoldCo, to be renamed Equitable Holdings at closing. Equitable shareholders will receive 1.55516 HoldCo shares for each Equitable share, while Corebridge shares convert 1-for-1; certain preferred shares will convert into like HoldCo series. Upon closing, Corebridge holders will own about 51% of HoldCo and Equitable holders about 49%, with the combined company expected to list its common and preferred shares on the NYSE. The combined entity will be led by Corebridge’s CEO as CEO of HoldCo, with Equitable’s CEO as Executive Chair, and will be headquartered in Houston.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Agreement and Plan of Merger (all-stock combination under HoldCo)
  • Counterparty: Corebridge Financial
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 26 2026 / same
  • Reason: Create scaled life and retirement leader with synergies

Original SEC Filing: Equitable Holdings, Inc. [ EQH ] - 8-K - Mar. 26, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Crypto Company Acquires Frame Blockchain Assets; Contingent Stock Payouts Up to $50.5 Million
data.description:
Crypto Company completed an asset acquisition of the Frame blockchain business, obtaining all related IP, code, and infrastructure. Consideration is structured as milestone-based stock issuances, starting with 2.5% of shares at sustained $100 million market cap and valuation thresholds and additional tranches with an aggregate potential value of about $50.5 million up to $1.0 billion milestones. The company must fund its subsidiary with at least $2.0 million within 120 days (extendable), and the deal includes a buy-sell provision tied to specified termination events. Alongside closing, the parties executed a Consulting Agreement for leadership and technical services and finalized IP Assignment and Bill of Sale documents to consolidate ownership and operations.

Agreement 1: Crypto Company Acquires Frame Blockchain Assets; Contingent Stock Payouts Up to $50.5 Million

  • Agreement type: Asset Purchase Agreement for Frame blockchain assets
  • Counterparty: Frame Holdings; Sean Docherty
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 20 2026 / Mar 20 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Expand into blockchain infrastructure with owned IP

Agreement 2: Crypto Company Inks Consulting Agreement With Frame Holdings at $20,000 Per Month

  • Agreement type: Consulting Agreement for executive and blockchain services
  • Counterparty: Frame Holdings
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 20 2026 / Mar 20 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Secure leadership and expertise to scale Frame

Agreement 3: Crypto Company Secures Full Rights to Frame IP via Assignment

  • Agreement type: Intellectual Property Assignment Agreement
  • Counterparty: Frame Holdings
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 20 2026 / Mar 20 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Consolidate ownership of core Frame blockchain IP

Agreement 4: Crypto Company Completes Transfer of Frame Assets via Bill of Sale

  • Agreement type: Bill of Sale and Assignment
  • Counterparty: Frame Holdings
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 20 2026 / Mar 20 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Effectuate transfer of all Frame operating assets

Original SEC Filing: Crypto Co [ CRCW ] - 8-K - Mar. 26, 2026

data.title: HWH International 2025 10-K: Revenue $0.87M, EPS (0.40)
data.description:
HWH International reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $0.867 million and a net loss per share of $(0.40), reflecting the winding down of its café operations that materially reduced food & beverage sales. Despite lower top-line results and a decline in gross profit, net loss improved slightly versus the prior year. The company completed a reverse merger and domicile change in 2025 and completed an equity offering to support its strategic shift toward digital initiatives.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0.867 million (down from $1.254 million in 2024)
  • Gross Profit: $0.460 million (down from $0.602 million in 2024)
  • Operating Income: $(3.189) million (operating expenses implied at $3,648,405)
  • Net Loss: $(2.658) million (improved from $(2.766) million in 2024)
  • Loss per Common Share (Basic & Diluted): $(0.40) (improved from $(0.76) in 2024)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Revenue declined year-over-year principally due to the closure of multiple Hapi Café locations in Singapore and Korea, reducing the company's F&B channel sales.
  • Channel Shift: The company is refocusing away from brick-and-mortar cafés toward digital initiatives and alternative food & beverage ventures.
  • New Initiatives: Launched the Hapi Marketplace with a planned U.S. launch and expansion to South Korea and Hong Kong, and is developing the Hapi Wealth Builder educational program.
  • Operational Milestones: Completed a business combination on January 9, 2024, and a reverse merger with a domicile change to Nevada on November 14, 2025; completed a 2025 equity offering to fund growth initiatives.
  • Brand & Strategy: Emphasis on a community-driven Hapi Café concept with cross-selling opportunities and phased regional rollouts for the marketplace and wealth education programs as the company repositions its operations.

Original SEC Filing: HWH International Inc. [ HWH ] - 10-K - Mar. 25, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: UK suffers OECD's biggest growth downgrade as Iran war pushes up energy costs
data.description:
By David Milliken

Britain's economic growth prospects this year received the sharpest downgrade of any major economy in the OECD's interim forecast update on Thursday following the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, while inflation is set to rise faster too.

The Paris-based international body cut its 2026 forecast for British economic growth by half a percentage point to 0.7%, compared with a 0.4 percentage point downgrade for the euro zone and a 0.3 percentage point upgrade for the United States.

"Planned fiscal tightening and higher energy prices are anticipated to keep growth subdued in the United Kingdom, though the impact will be attenuated by lower policy rates next year," the OECD said in its report.

Following are further highlights from the report and other context:

  • Britain's growth forecast for 2027 is unchanged at 1.3%

  • Britain's inflation forecast for 2026 is revised up by 1.5 percentage points from December to 4.0%, the biggest upward revision of any large, advanced economy

  • UK inflation in 2027 is forecast to be 2.6%, 0.5 percentage points higher than in December and above the Bank of England's 2% target

  • Poorer UK households spend more on gas and electricity than in other rich countries, though total energy spending makes up a smaller share of UK inflation than elsewhere

  • The OECD expects the BoE to keep interest rates unchanged this year then cut in Q1 2027 as inflation eases

  • Britain's Office for Budget Responsibility, in forecasts finalised just before the start of the conflict, predicted GDP growth of 1.1% this year and 1.6% in 2027

  • The BoE this month forecast inflation would rise to 3.0-3.5% over the next couple of quarters

  • Prime Minister Keir Starmer has made boosting growth and reducing the cost of living top goals for his government

  • Finance minister Rachel Reeves said the forecasts showed the war in the Middle East was affecting Britain but she would still focus on "regional growth, embracing AI and innovation, and  establishing  a closer relationship with the EU"
